questions.question_text: Every real estate advertisement in Pennsylvania must include the employing broker's:
questions.explanation: PA requires every advertisement to include the employing broker's business name AND telephone number. Both must be equal in size to the agent's name and number in the advertisement.

questions.question_text: An agent's yard sign displays the agent's name in large letters and the broker's name in small print below. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation — sizes must be equal
distractors: Acceptable if broker name is visible
questions.explanation: The broker's name and phone number must be equal in size to the agent's name and number. Having the broker's name in smaller print violates PA advertising regulations.

questions.question_text: The broker name requirement in Pennsylvania advertising applies to which media?
questions.correct_answer: All media including print and digital
questions.explanation: The requirement applies to ALL advertising media: print, internet, social media, yard signs, business cards, email signatures, and any other form of real estate advertising.

questions.question_text: A salesperson creates a personal business card that omits the broker's phone number. The salesperson:
distractors: Is compliant if the broker name appears
Needs only a license number shown
May omit the phone for business cards
questions.explanation: Business cards are considered advertising and must include the broker's business name AND telephone number, both equal in size to the agent's information. Omitting the phone number is a violation.

questions.question_text: A broker's advertisement prominently displays the brokerage name but uses a disconnected phone number. This is:
distractors: Acceptable if the name is correct
questions.explanation: The broker must include a working telephone number. A disconnected number fails to meet the contact information requirement of PA advertising regulations.

questions.question_text: A real estate team name in Pennsylvania must include:
questions.correct_answer: At least one licensed member's name and 'team'
questions.explanation: PA requires team names to include at least one licensed team member's name and the word 'team.' The broker's name must also still appear prominently in the advertising.

questions.question_text: An unlicensed office assistant appears in a team advertisement as a team member. This is:
questions.explanation: Unlicensed assistants may not appear in advertising as team members. Only licensed individuals can be presented as part of a real estate team in Pennsylvania advertising.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to use a nickname in their advertising. Under PA rules, the licensee must:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania allows the use of nicknames in advertising only if the nickname is registered with PREC on the licensee's license record. This ensures consumers can identify the actual licensee.

questions.question_text: The 'Smith Home Group' advertises without including the word 'team' and without listing any individual licensee names. This advertising is:
distractors: Acceptable as a group brand name
Legal if the broker approves it
Compliant if the broker name appears
questions.explanation: PA team advertising requires the word 'team' and at least one licensed member's name. Using 'Group' instead of 'team' and omitting individual names violates these requirements.

questions.question_text: In team advertising, the employing broker's name must be:
distractors: Hidden in the terms and conditions
Shown only on the team website
questions.explanation: Even in team advertising, the broker's name must appear prominently. The team identity does not replace or diminish the broker identification requirement.

questions.question_text: On a social media property post, the broker's name must be:
distractors: Accessible via a link to the broker's website
Available in the agent's profile bio only
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania does NOT follow a 'one-click rule.' The broker's name must be visible directly on every page, post, or advertisement — not merely accessible through a link or profile page.

questions.question_text: Pennsylvania's approach to internet advertising and broker identification is:
questions.correct_answer: Broker name visible on every page/post
questions.explanation: PA requires the broker's name to be visible on EVERY page or post. It does NOT follow the one-click rule used by some other states, which would allow broker identification to be accessible via a link.

questions.question_text: An agent creates a real estate Instagram page. The broker's name and phone number must appear:
distractors: Only in the account's bio section
Only in the first post of the account
Only when a consumer requests it
questions.explanation: Every individual advertising post must include the broker's business name and phone number. Having broker information only in the bio or first post does not satisfy Pennsylvania's per-post requirement.

questions.question_text: An agent's email signature includes their name and phone number but omits the broker's information. This email signature is:
distractors: Acceptable if broker info is on the website
questions.explanation: Email signatures are considered advertising in Pennsylvania. They must include the broker's business name and telephone number, equal in size to the agent's information.

questions.question_text: An agent posts a property video on YouTube. The broker's name must be:
questions.correct_answer: Visible in the video or description
distractors: Only in the channel's About section
questions.explanation: All advertising media, including video content on platforms like YouTube, must include the broker's name and contact information. It should be visible within the video itself or the post/description.
